title: Salesforce Activity, Calendar, and Matter Channel backend-first contract
salesforce_visible_capabilities: Salesforce task and event activity model

current_ui_boundary: Matter > activity uses mounted activity create/patch routes and timeline read route
Matter > calendar uses mounted calendar create/patch and deadline confirmation routes
Matter > Channel uses mounted internal message route and provider-sync route-backed blocked state
External provider send/publish controls remain absent until owner approval and provider receipts exist

route_policy_contract.service_boundary: Read facade over MatterTimelineEvent, MatterTask, MatterCalendarEvent, and channel metadata; returns safe records only
Create task/event/call/email-log activity and append a permission-trimmed timeline event; no email send or external calendar mutation
Allowlisted activity status, due date, assignee, and note metadata updates only; raw body and external provider identifiers stay hidden
Read MatterCalendarEvent records with legal consequence and reminder fields only after model migration; provider event ids omitted
Create internal Matter calendar event and audit evidence; Microsoft Graph or provider mutation requires separate owner-approved provider gate
Use changeMatterDeadline with critical-deadline dual-control for legal consequence changes
Deadline board projection over MatterCalendarEvent and MatterTask with count-leak prevention
Critical deadline change confirmation through confirmCriticalDeadlineChange; requester and confirmer must differ
Metadata-only thread and message projection; raw provider payloads, attachments, and direct personal contact identifiers omitted
Create internal message draft/log only; external Slack/Teams/email send requires provider receipt and human approval
Return route-backed provider_blocked state and audit evidence; does not send to Slack, Teams, Outlook, or external calendars

safe_output_rules: raw body, raw query, storage path, and direct provider payloads omitted
provider_event_id omitted unless provider owner gate resolves
critical deadline changes require dual control
client-visible calendar entries require explicit review flag
external send actions blocked until provider receipt exists

verification_cases.requirement: GET /api/matters/:id/activities returns task, event, email-log, call, and channel metadata projections with denied/review_required envelopes and no hidden counts
POST /api/matters/:id/activities creates an internal activity, appends timeline and audit evidence, supports idempotency, and blocks email send/calendar provider shortcuts
PATCH /api/matters/:id/activities/:activityId updates only allowlisted fields and blocks raw body/provider payload edits
GET /api/matters/:id/calendar-events returns internal calendar events with safe legal deadline metadata and no provider event ids
POST /api/matters/:id/calendar-events creates an internal calendar event, records audit evidence, supports idempotency, and does not mutate external calendars
PATCH /api/matters/:id/calendar-events/:eventId enforces allowlisted date/status changes and dual control for critical legal deadline changes
GET /api/matters/:id/deadlines returns a permission-trimmed deadline board projection without raw source refs
POST /api/matters/:id/deadlines/:deadlineId/confirm-change requires different requester/confirmer principals and emits audit evidence
GET /api/matters/:id/channel returns metadata-only Matter Channel threads/messages without raw provider payloads or direct personal contact identifiers
POST /api/matters/:id/channel/messages creates an internal message draft/log, blocks external send by default, records audit evidence, and supports idempotency
POST /api/matters/:id/channel/provider-sync returns route-backed provider_blocked state, audit evidence, no provider credentials, and no raw provider payload
Matter UI exposes activity/calendar/channel write controls only when backed by mounted API routes; external provider effects remain blocked/configuration state until owner/provider receipts exist

evidence_gates: scripts/validate-sf-client-matter-parity-crosswalk.mjs verifies this contract exists, W03 UI controls are route-backed, and external provider send controls remain absent
packages/matter/src/activity-calendar-channel-service.js implements activity, calendar, deadline, channel, audit, timeline, and provider-blocked safe response boundaries
apps/api/test/sf-b-w03-activity-calendar-channel.test.js covers mounted API route descriptors, activity write/patch/replay, calendar approval/confirm, channel message, and provider-blocked state
Playwright Matter activity/calendar/channel QA proves real route-backed controls, safe output, and no raw provider data in UI exposure
Hermes MCP remains check-only and cannot authorize production, deployment, final approval, or enterprise trust claims

owner_decisions.decision: Activity type taxonomy for task, event, call, email-log, note, and channel-message must be approved before provider or client-visible workflow expansion
Critical deadline dual-control policy and legal consequence labels must be approved before external calendar provider enablement
Matter Channel provider strategy must choose internal-only, Slack, Teams, Outlook, or hybrid before provider sync can leave blocked state

blocked_until: External provider owner decisions and receipts exist for calendar/channel sync
Microsoft Graph, Slack, Teams, or Outlook provider credentials are configured through an approved provider boundary
Provider smoke tests prove no raw provider payload or direct personal contact identifier exposure
Human owner approval authorizes provider enablement; this contract does not authorize production, deployment, final approval, or enterprise trust claims
